# Load the provided text file and display its contents and indices for reference
path = "/mnt/data/text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()

print("Text length:", len(text))
print(text)

# Let's extract all occurrences that look like citations: content inside {...} and the bracket [surah: aya]
import re

# Pattern to capture: {...} [surah: aya]
pattern = re.compile(r"(\{[^}]*\})\s*\[([^\]:]+):\s*([0-9]+)\]", re.UNICODE)

matches = list(pattern.finditer(text))
extracted = []
for m in matches:
    span_start = m.start(1)  # start of the {...}
    span_end = m.end(3) + (m.end() - m.start() - (m.end(3)-m.start(3)))  # not necessary; we want only the braces span
    # Actually per schema we need the span of the full citation text we will validate (the phrase inside {}), 
    # plus we also need to record numbers. We'll record the {} span.
    extracted.append({
        "brace_span_start": m.start(1),
        "brace_span_end": m.end(1),
        "brace_text": m.group(1),
        "surah": m.group(2).strip(),
        "aya_number": int(m.group(3)),
        "full_span_start": m.start(),
        "full_span_end": m.end(),
        "full_text": m.group(0)
    })

extracted

# Since the file wasn't found, we'll reconstruct the text from the user's message for precise indexing.
text = """الإجابة على السؤال هي أهل الكتاب، وهم قوم نوح، وهم الذين كذبوا رسوله نوح، ورفضوا الدعوة إلى التوبة والتوحيد.
قال الله تعالى: {وَقَالَ لَهُمْ نُوحٌ أَلَا تَتُوبُونَ إِلَى اللَّهِ يَا بَنِي آدَمَ أَنَّهُ هُوَ رَبُّكُمْ أَرَضَانَ أَنْ تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَتُخْرِجُوا مِنْهَا دُونَ اللَّهِ سِبْيًا أَأَنتُم بِهَذَا ظَالِمُونَ} [الفرقان: 24]
وَقَالَ لَهُمْ نُوحٌ أَلَا تَتَخْشَعُونَ قَالَ رَبِّ إِنَّهُمْ عَصَوْنَ وَاتَّبَعُوا أَهْوَاءَهُمْ وَكُلُ أُمَّةٍ بِنَصْبِهَا أَجَزَاءُ ضَنِينٌ} [هود: 69]
قال الله تعالى: {قَالَ رَبِّ إِنَّ قَوْمِيْ تَخْدَعُونَ} [هود: 89]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا فِرْعَوْنَ إِلَى بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلَى} [الغاشية: 24]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَإِن جَزِبْنَاهُمْ حَتّى إِذَا جَزِبْنَاهُمْ بَخِيلٌ كَأَن لَمْ نَرَوْهُمْ وَدَخَلْنَاهُمْ بَخِيلًا كَأَن لَمْ نَخْلُقْهُمْ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَغَى فِي الْعِلْمِ وَاتَّبَعُوا لَهْوَ قَلْبِهِمْ وَزَعَزَعَهُمُ الْبَخِلاءُ} [الفرقان: 20]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَقَالَ لَهُمْ أَتَعْرِفُونَ اللَّهَ وَهُوَ يُزَيِّلُكُمْ لَهُ دِينُكُمْ وَلَوْ شَاءَ لَرَفَعَكُمْ تَضْوَلاً} [الفرقان: 3]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ رَسُولًا مِنْهُمْ فَقَالُوا لَوْ يَطَاعُ مِنْهُمْ لَأَطَعْنَا} [الفرقان: 7]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ رَسُولًا مِنْهُمْ فَقَالُوا لَوْ يَطَاعُ مِنْهُمْ لَأَطَعْنَا} [الفرقان: 7]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ رَسُولًا مِنْهُمْ فَقَالُوا لَوْ يَطَاعُ مِنْهُمْ لَأَطَعْنَا} [الفرقان: 7]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ رَسُولًا مِنْهُمْ فَقَالُوا لَوْ يَطَاعُ مِنْهُمْ لَأَطَعْنَا} [الفرقان: 7]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ رَسُولًا مِنْهُمْ فَقَالُوا لَوْ يَطَاعُ مِنْهُمْ لَأَطَعْنَا} [الفرقان: 7]
وقال الله تعالى: {وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ رَسُولًا مِنْهُمْ فَقَالُوا لَوْ"""

print("Text length:", len(text))
print(text)

import re
pattern = re.compile(r"(\{[^}]*\})\s*\[([^\]:]+):\s*([0-9]+)\]", re.UNICODE)
matches = list(pattern.finditer(text))
len(matches), [m.group(0) for m in matches]

output.code:
# Build the citation list with spans from the regex matches we already have
import re

pattern = re.compile(r"(\{[^}]*\})\s*\[([^\]:]+):\s*([0-9]+)\]", re.UNICODE)
matches = list(pattern.finditer(text))

citations = []
for m in matches:
    brace_span_start = m.start(1)
    brace_span_end = m.end(1)
    brace_text = m.group(1)
    surah = m.group(2).strip()
    aya_number = int(m.group(3))
    full_span_start = m.start()
    full_span_end = m.end()
    full_text = m.group(0)
    citations.append({
        "brace_span_start": brace_span_start,
        "brace_span_end": brace_span_end,
        "brace_text": brace_text,
        "surah": surah,
        "aya_number": aya_number,
        "full_span_start": full_span_start,
        "full_span_end": full_span_end,
        "full_text": full_text
    })

len(citations), citations[:2]

# Adjust spans to exclude the curly braces per schema instruction
adjusted = []
for c in citations:
    inner_start = c["brace_span_start"] + 1  # skip '{'
    inner_end = c["brace_span_end"] - 1      # before '}'
    inner_text = text[inner_start:inner_end]
    adjusted.append({
        "span_start": inner_start,
        "span_end": inner_end,
        "span_text": inner_text,
        "surah": c["surah"],
        "aya_number": c["aya_number"],
        "full_span_start": c["full_span_start"],
        "full_span_end": c["full_span_end"],
        "full_text": c["full_text"]
    })

len(adjusted), adjusted[0]

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
